content.text: FEATURES.md updates:

input.old_string: The nightly now deterministically restores the fork's exact workflow set mid-merge (`git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows`) BEFORE conflict detection, so upstream workflow additions/edits never enter the fork, never reach the AI resolver, and never hit the push restriction. Wanting an upstream workflow = deliberate local commit with a user token | `(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED)` |
input.new_string:
The nightly now deterministically restores the fork's exact workflow set mid-merge (`git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows`) BEFORE conflict detection, so upstream workflow additions/edits never enter the fork, never reach the AI resolver, and never hit the push restriction — and the dropped upstream CI delta is SURFACED, not silently erased (job summary + log + handed to the semantic review, which appends advisory `CI-NOTE:` lines when upstream moves build-relevant logic into its CI), with a pre-commit postcondition proving the committed tree carries zero workflow-dir changes. Wanting an upstream workflow = deliberate local commit with a user token | `(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED)` |
| Nightly pipeline integrity | three cheap deterministic guards on the merge→build→advance hand-offs: a fatal (non-conflict) `git merge` failure is caught via missing MERGE_HEAD instead of masquerading as a clean merge (the `set +e` wrapper accepts every nonzero exit); the build + advance jobs consume the merge commit's immutable SHA — not the mutable `nightly/*` branch name — under a workflow-level `concurrency` group (queue, never cancel), and advance refuses to fast-forward main if the candidate ref moved; `gh release create` tags the exact built commit via `--target` + a post-publish remote-tag assert (without `--target` gh tags the DEFAULT branch tip, which during a nightly is pre-advance main — every nightly-published release tag pointed one commit behind the installer's true source until 2026-07-11) | `(NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY)` |

| Nightly pipeline integrity | three cheap deterministic guards on the merge→build→advance hand-offs: a fatal (non-conflict) `git merge` failure is caught via missing MERGE_HEAD instead of masquerading as a clean merge (the `set +e` wrapper accepts every nonzero exit); the build + advance jobs consume the merge commit's immutable SHA — not the mutable `nightly/*` branch name — under a workflow-level `concurrency` group (queue, never cancel), and advance refuses to fast-forward main if the candidate ref moved; `gh release create` tags the exact built commit via `--target` + a post-publish remote-tag assert (without `--target` gh tags the DEFAULT branch tip, which during a nightly is pre-advance main — every nightly-published release tag pointed one commit behind the installer's true source until 2026-07-11) | `(NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY)` |

lines: | Idle teammates green deterministically | Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's background_tasks[] as `status:"running"` FOREVER (captured live: 33 teammate entries, all "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had all finished hours earlier) — and the (BG-STALE) reap only runs inside hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while the set was <15 min idle latched yellow indefinitely (nothing ever re-evaluated; 9 terminals observed stuck). superset-notify.py now keeps a per-terminal teammate LEDGER parsed incrementally from the lead session transcript (payload `transcript_path`; offset+state cached in `<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, keyed by transcript path so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an `idle_notification` teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when EVERY tracked teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped BEFORE the split/snapshot/hold decision — the lead greens (or blues on a shell remainder) at its actual final turn end instead of holding forever. Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates seen / any parse error / any teammate not idle → entries kept (old behaviour + (BG-STALE) unchanged behind it); raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate (forks have real hook lifecycles and would never idle-notify); subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through untouched; bg_ids marker reconciliation still uses the RAW list. Decision log carries a `[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]` suffix for auditability | `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)` |

| ReferenceError-class build gate | the build (and therefore every nightly, whose build job calls the same workflow) fails on cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics ONLY (TS2304/2552/2662/2663/18004) — bare identifiers esbuild bundles fine but that throw ReferenceError at runtime, exactly what a CLEAN-merging upstream hunk can leave in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx incident: upstream renamed the workspace source variable in a non-conflicting hunk; fork's cache-first hold kept referencing it; every v2 workspace view crashed). Per-package direct tsc (turbo graph is debt-blocked); the accepted type debt has zero of these codes so the gate can't false-abort | `(REFERR-GATE)` |

| Nightly dependency-consistency + lock regen | the nightly merge can introduce dependency-state drift that only the build discovers (after a windows-arm64 runner spins up): a stale fork root `overrides` entry that pins a package to a version different from the merged workspace pin (electron-builder "production dependency not found …"), or a `bun.lock` left stale by an upstream package.json bump (frozen-install fails). The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). No full `--frozen-lockfile` install runs here: the merge runner is linux, so a platform-specific install quirk would be a false abort, and the regenerated lock is consistent with the manifests by construction. The fork carries NO mastra `overrides` and pins `@mastra/core`/`mastracode` to upstream's exact versions in all three workspaces (upstream keeps mastra consistent via identical exact pins; the seed commit's redundant override + a recovery merge that resolved the pins DOWN to 0.18.0 were the recurring breakage, both corrected) | `(LOCK-REGEN)` |

-| Fork-owned workflow dir | `.github/workflows/` carries ONLY the fork's own workflows — upstream CI files were dropped at fork creation (they target upstream's infra/secrets) and the Actions `GITHUB_TOKEN` can NEVER push a workflow-file change anyway (no `workflows` scope exists for it: "refusing to allow a GitHub App to create or update workflow"). Upstream desktop-v1.14.3 added `deploy-relay.yml`, which merged CLEANLY and then killed the candidate push after every gate had already passed. +# (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) Never let two nightly instances interleave: an overlapping
+# schedule + manual dispatch can delete/replace the mutable candidate branch
+# between one run's merge, build checkout and advance. Queue, never cancel — a
+# running merge/build must finish or fail on its own.
+concurrency:
+  group: nightly-merge
+  cancel-in-progress: false
+

     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
@@ -41,6 +49,7 @@ jobs:

The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). (none)

All Codex findings triaged and the batch is live. Final summary:

**Codex 5.6 (xhigh) verdict on my original fix**: root cause correct, `checkout --no-overlay` sound, fork-owned-dir policy preferable to a deploy key — but "pipeline not yet sufficient overall". It found 4 real adjacent holes, all now closed in `73cf99224`:

1. **BLOCKER (confirmed live)** — every nightly-published release tag pointed one commit behind the built installer: `gh release create` without `--target` tags the default branch, which during a nightly is pre-advance main. Fixed with `--target <built commit>` + a post-publish remote-tag assert; I also retargeted the live `desktop-v1.14.3` tag to the true build commit `848414f7f`.
2. **HIGH** — a fatal (non-conflict) `git merge` failure could masquerade as a clean merge under the `set +e` wrapper and commit a bogus single-parent "merge" with a bumped baseline. Now verified via `MERGE_HEAD`.
3. **HIGH** — overlapping nightly runs could swap the mutable `nightly/*` candidate branch between merge, build, and advance. Now: workflow-level `concurrency` group (queue, never cancel), build + advance consume the immutable merge SHA, advance refuses if the candidate ref moved.
4. **HIGH** — dropping upstream workflow changes silently created an audit blind spot (upstream sometimes moves build-relevant logic into CI). Now the dropped delta is printed to the job summary/log and handed to the semantic reviewer, which appends advisory `CI-NOTE:` lines; plus a pre-commit postcondition proving zero workflow-dir changes in the committed tree (so even a misbehaving AI resolver can't resurrect one). I chose surface-and-continue over Codex's fail-closed acknowledgement — fail-closed would stall the autonomous nightly on every routine upstream CI tweak.

Declined per your earlier answer: issue-on-failure alerting (GitHub emails cover it). Skipped: branch-protection interactions (none on this personal repo; the rebuild-in-place design self-heals a failed advance).

Meanwhile the rerun went fully green: **v1.14.3 merged, built, published, baseline advanced** — merge job proved the WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED step in production (deploy-relay.yml dropped, push succeeded). Marker gate sim + YAML validation + /code-review low all clean on the hardening batch; it takes effect from tonight's nightly. Full Codex report: `tmp/llm-responses/codex-nightly-workflow-fix-advice-11-07-26---12-17-44.md`.
